# Transcrição: 18-How to Map Out Funnel

## [00:00 - 03:02]

Okay, so one of the big important steps in running any kind of client account, including in e-commerce or coaching, is to start mapping out the funnel that we're going to be using for this client. Now there's a couple different options in terms of funnel. Even if we're rapid fire testing, it's always worth mapping out the funnel that we're eventually going to be using, okay? So for example, if you're using gamification, you can test this out using lead ads, although you're going to have to build out that funnel afterwards. A lead ad is not really going to work with that kind of structure. So what we do is we use Lucidchart, however there's lots of different tools out there that you can use, and you can also access these templates as well. And so this is one of the standard, this is a pretty typical lead gen funnel. This pretty much works across any and all of them. There's a separate funnel for gamification. So if you are running gamification as a hook, that would be the one to use. This is a standard lead gen where you have an offer or a hook, and so you're sending them to that offer page. So what we have here is our two campaigns. So we're going to have our stage one and our stage two, top of funnel targeting cold audience, and then bottom of funnel targeting warm audience. This is probably more like middle of funnel, but that's okay. And we've got success metrics along each step as well. So at the top of the funnel, we've got our Facebook ad. It's going to be run as a conversion objective when we activate the funnel. What we're looking for here is a 1% click through rate and a $3 or less cost per click. The reason it's important to put these metrics in place is so that your team understands where they should be tracking. And if when they're basically reporting on the funnel, they know exactly where potential bottlenecks are happening based on if the numbers are matching up to those success metrics or not. So for example, if we have a minimum 20% conversion rate required on our offer page, but the team starts to you start to track the funnel and you realize your conversion rate is only, you know, 10% then you know that your bottleneck is happening here. So it's important to map out these metrics here. So typically have a look at the industry standard conversion rates for metrics here. But typically your landing page should convert at a minimum of 20%. You should be getting a 1% click through rate on top of funnel. This may be a bit lower if you're in a dry, dry industry like insurance or mortgage brokers. But then bottom of funnel should be a lot higher as well because this is a warm audience. And then you start to say like what kind of audiences you're going to be testing out here. Okay. And who will you

## [03:02 - 04:10]

be excluding? Here we have a bit of a breakdown on what happens. So we've got our offer page they opt in, then there's a thank you page. We've got our standard event lead. Again, we map this out so that at any one time, our team can access this through the Google Drive folder. And they will be able to find really quickly what events are being used throughout the funnel instead of having to go through it themselves. Right. We also are going to map out the email sequences that we're going to be setting up for the client. So this should be number five. So typically the way that it runs for lead gen is you have one email that's sent immediately, you can connect this to SMS. So if you choose to collect mobile information, you can also do messenger. So that's another option as well. But essentially, you're going to send an email immediately with access details to the offer, recap the benefits, recap why they should, you know, claim it and build some urgency in there. That's really important. If you do have SMS, you will find a much higher open rate and typically
